todaysPlan.activity: Homework Spelling – Schwa Sounds And High-frequency Words
todaysPlan.reason: Active uploaded homework is "Benchmark Advance Spelling Unit 9 Week 2", so today's required work must anchor on Schwa sound (ə), Long vowel sounds (i, o, a), Antonyms, High-frequency vocabulary before broader profile goals.
todaysPlan.method: Read the assignment-aligned content, ask evidence-based checks, then use profile motivators only as framing.
probeSequence: Read or preview the assignment content
Ask why it happened using text evidence
Have the child explain the concept back

childProfile: Ila is an 8-year-old with ADHD (combined type) and dyslexia who learns best through visual-motor game formats with immediate feedback. She demonstrates 100% spelling accuracy in timed competitive games (Monster Stampede, Wheel of Fortune) but collapses in isolated letter-selection tasks (Spell-Check 7-47% accuracy with frustration). Her CELF-5 shows strengths in Sentence Comprehension (63rd percentile WITH pictures) but severe deficits in Following Directions (2nd percentile—cannot hold multi-step sequences), Recalling Sentences (5th percentile—auditory memory breakdown), and Word Structure (5th percentile—oral morphology errors on plurals, tenses, pronouns). She is highly motivated by competitive scoring, responds well to praise, and needs frequent movement breaks. Sustained attention ceiling is 10-15 minutes before cognitive load triggers frustration or shutdown. She avoids phoneme segmentation drills (52+ days of active avoidance) but engages with whole-word orthographic pattern instruction. Friendly, willing, and explicitly requests help when stuck ('Can I have a help?'), but anxiety about being wrong can trigger withdrawal ('She can't do it here'). Visual support is non-negotiable—performance drops from 100% with pictures to 37.5% without. Never assume she can decode new words phonetically; she relies on sight vocabulary and morphological pattern recognition.
stopAfter: After the active homework check is complete, or earlier if frustration is rising.
rewardPolicy: Reward only after the active homework is checked; broader review goals belong in review mode.
